primary_accounting_issue: Possibilités de documentation de couverture en comptes consolidés pour le risque de change lié à un dividende intragroupe comptabilisé en créance

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the stated precedence and governing-lineage rule by selecting IFRS 9 as the single governing standard for hedge-accounting models and hedged-item eligibility, while IAS 21 is retained only as supporting authority because it clarifies when intragroup foreign-currency exposures are not fully eliminated in consolidation.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — the hedge-accounting question is directly governed by IFRS 9, and the remaining uncertainty is only about which specific hedge model might fit the fact pattern rather than which standard governs.

primary_authority.reason: This standard directly governs hedge accounting models and whether intragroup items can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: This standard clarifies that intragroup monetary items can generate exchange differences not fully eliminated on consolidation, which supports the identification of hedgeable FX exposure.
This standard helps frame the underlying dividend as a recognized item in separate financial statements before considering its treatment in consolidation.
This standard supports the characterization of dividends and distributions, helping distinguish owner distributions from liability-related amounts.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for a recognized foreign-currency monetary exposure that remains exposed in consolidation
Hedge accounting for a highly probable forecast intragroup foreign-currency transaction affecting consolidated profit or loss
Hedge accounting for foreign-currency exposure linked to a net investment in a foreign operation

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies recognized assets and liabilities as possible hedged items, which captures a recognized dividend receivable if the FX exposure remains relevant in consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately permits highly probable forecast transactions, including certain intragroup foreign-currency transactions, as hedged items when they affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for foreign-currency exposures.
